---
title: "Preparation"
format: html
---

::: {.column-page}

```{mermaid}
%%{init: {'gantt': {'axisFormat': '%Y-%m-%d'}}}%%
gantt

    title Big Data & Analytics — Prep plan (everything done by 2026-03-09)
    dateFormat YYYY-MM-DD
    axisFormat %Y-%m-%d
    excludes weekends, 2026-02-16, 2026-02-17, 2026-02-18, 2026-02-19, 2026-02-20

    section Constraints
    Vacation :vac, 2026-02-16, 2026-02-20
    Prep complete (T-2 weeks) :milestone, dl, 2026-03-09, 0d
    First teaching day (Sessions 1–2) :milestone, teach, 2026-03-23, 0d

    section Preparation by area
    Foundations (Session 1) :found, 2026-01-13, 3d
    Structured Data (Sessions 2–6) :struct, after found, 10d
    Big Data (Sessions 7–9) :big, after struct, 6d
    Organizations (Sessions 10–11) :org, after big, 4d
    Integration & polish (all links) :buf, after org, 3d
```
:::

::: {.callout-important title="Materials"}
See **/home/gerit/workspace/projects/bda/nextcloud** (lecture_x)
:::

## Preparation overview

- [x] Select contents for each session
- [ ] Find contents: Exploratory data analysis
- [ ] Find contents: Regression I
- [ ] Find contents: Regression II
- [ ] Find contents: Machine learning I
- [ ] Find contents: Machine learning II
- [ ] Find contents: Analytics case examples

- [ ] Check: Jupyter Exercise setup ?? (GitHub?)


### Session 0 — Course logistics
- [ ] Slides polished
- [ ] Teaching notes prepared

---

### Session 1 — Foundations of data analytics
- [x] Learning objectives set
- [x] Lecture contents selected
- [x] Exercise contents selected
- [ ] Lecture polished
- [ ] Exercise polished
- [ ] Exercise tested
- [ ] Teaching notes prepared

---

### Session 2 — Structuring and preparing data
- [x] Learning objectives set
- [x] Lecture contents selected
- [ ] Exercise contents selected
- [ ] Lecture polished
- [ ] Exercise polished
- [ ] Exercise tested
- [ ] Teaching notes prepared

---

### Session 3 — Exploratory data analysis
- [ ] Learning objectives set
- [ ] Lecture contents selected ⚠️
- [ ] Exercise contents selected
- [ ] Lecture polished
- [ ] Exercise polished
- [ ] Exercise tested
- [ ] Teaching notes prepared

---

### Session 4 — Regression I
- [ ] Learning objectives set
- [ ] Lecture contents selected ⚠️
- [ ] Exercise contents selected
- [ ] Lecture polished
- [ ] Exercise polished
- [ ] Exercise tested
- [ ] Teaching notes prepared

---

### Session 5 — Regression II
- [ ] Learning objectives set
- [ ] Lecture contents selected (**slides available**)
- [ ] Exercise contents selected
- [ ] Lecture polished
- [ ] Exercise polished
- [ ] Exercise tested
- [ ] Teaching notes prepared

---

### Session 6 — Machine learning I
- [ ] Learning objectives set
- [ ] Lecture contents selected ⚠️
- [ ] Exercise contents selected
- [ ] Lecture polished
- [ ] Exercise polished
- [ ] Exercise tested
- [ ] Teaching notes prepared

---

### Session 7 — Machine learning II
- [ ] Learning objectives set
- [ ] Lecture contents selected (**slides available**)
- [ ] Exercise contents selected
- [ ] Lecture polished
- [ ] Exercise polished
- [ ] Exercise tested
- [ ] Teaching notes prepared

---

### Session 8 — Big Data
- [ ] Learning objectives set
- [ ] Lecture contents selected ⚠️
- [ ] Exercise contents selected
- [ ] Lecture polished
- [ ] Exercise polished
- [ ] Exercise tested
- [ ] Teaching notes prepared

---

### Session 9 — Analytics case examples
- [ ] Learning objectives set
- [ ] Lecture contents selected ⚠️
- [ ] Exercise contents selected
- [ ] Lecture polished
- [ ] Exercise polished
- [ ] Exercise tested
- [ ] Teaching notes prepared

---

### Session 10 — Governance of data analytics
- [ ] Learning objectives set
- [ ] Lecture contents selected ⚠️
- [ ] Exercise contents selected
- [ ] Lecture polished
- [ ] Exercise polished
- [ ] Exercise tested
- [ ] Teaching notes prepared

---

### Session 11 — Recap, exam preparation, Q&A
- [ ] Learning objectives set
- [ ] Recap structure defined
- [ ] Exam-relevant topics consolidated
- [ ] Slides polished
- [ ] Teaching notes prepared

---

## Cross-cutting tasks

- [ ] Group work
- [ ] We may prepare the [handout](all_lectures.pdf) generated based on all slides.
- [ ] Exams: [fs-ise/big-data-analytics-exams](https://github.com/fs-ise/big-data-analytics-exams)
- [ ] Create first-slide background illustrations for all sessions (consistent design)
- [ ] Align learning objectives in individual sessions with the [overall overview](#learning-objectives)
- [ ] Link checker set up (slides / notes / exercises)
- [ ] Exercises run end-to-end (Dataset availability verified)
- [ ] Group exercises tested
- [ ] Test exam prepared
- [ ] Consistency of terminology across sessions
- [ ] Learning objectives aligned with exam scope
- [ ] Optional: handout / combined PDF prepared
- [ ] Syllabus updated
- [ ] License, code of conduct, contributing, ...


## TBD:

- [ ] Test exercise sheets: [GitHub jupyterlab](https://docs.github.com/en/codespaces/developing-in-a-codespace/getting-started-with-github-codespaces-for-machine-learning?utm_source=chatgpt.com#opening-your-codespace-in-jupyterlab) with pre-installation of pandas etc. and PDF generation
TBD: exercise sheets in a separate repository?
- [ ] Excerpts for teaching hub
- [ ] Exercise sheets: solutions?!

- [ ] Update requirements (exam):
    - show and read code for the analysis
    - include code reading and interpretation, maybe even pseudo-code writing in the exam (and exercises)
    - surveys during the lecture: vote for "additional exercise/more difficult exercise"

Work through the different parts (easier to identify materials for a part instead of comparing multiple lectures for multiple topics)

Slides:

- [ ] start with teaching notes and schedule. Setup teaching-notes
- [ ] screen and include relevant materials (e.g., Hopf, Prester; identify gaps)
- [ ] specify learning goals
- [ ] Prepare and test-run

Exercise sheets:

- [ ] Specify exercise goals and state typical exam questions
- [ ] Identify suitable exercise materials (rossbach, hopf, prester)
- [ ] Prepare and test-run
- [ ] Setup teaching-notes

- [ ] Split Data Analytics with Python.pdf and -> jnb

https://www.coursera.org/learn/data-analysis-with-python
- python, exploratory data analysis
-> accessible (good examples?!)
